notes: All questions are original and aligned with SAT standards.
Questions cover Math (Algebra, Advanced Math, Geometry, Statistics) and Reading & Writing domains.
Visual questions include visual_spec and visual_data for charts, tables, and graphs.
Passage-based questions share passage_id for grouped comprehension.

questions.stem: If 7x - 15 = 41, what is the value of x?
questions.explanation: Add 15 to both sides: 7x = 56. Divide by 7: x = 8.

questions.stem: What value of x satisfies the equation 4(x - 3) = 2x + 8?
questions.explanation: Expand: 4x - 12 = 2x + 8. Subtract 2x: 2x - 12 = 8. Add 12: 2x = 20, so x = 10.

questions.stem: Which values of x satisfy the inequality 3x - 7 > 2x + 5?
questions.explanation: Subtract 2x: x - 7 > 5. Add 7: x > 12.

questions.stem: If 3x + 2y = 19 and x - y = 2, what is the value of y?
questions.explanation: From the second equation, x = y + 2. Substitute into first: 3(y + 2) + 2y = 19, 3y + 6 + 2y = 19, 5y = 13, y = 3.

questions.stem: For what value of k will the system 4x + ky = 12 and 2x + 3y = 6 have infinitely many solutions?
questions.explanation: The equations must be multiples. Multiply the second by 2: 4x + 6y = 12. So k = 6.

questions.stem: A line passes through points (2, 7) and (4, 11). What is the slope of this line?
What is the y-intercept of the line with equation y = -3x + 9?
questions.explanation: When x = 0, y = 9, so the y-intercept is (0, 9) with value 9.

questions.stem: A recipe calls for 3 cups of flour for every 2 cups of sugar. How many cups of flour are needed for 8 cups of sugar?
questions.explanation: Set up proportion: 3/2 = x/8. Cross-multiply: 24 = 2x, x = 12.

questions.stem: A shirt originally priced at $45 is on sale for $36. What is the percent discount?
A population of 2,400 increases by 15% one year, then decreases by 10% the next. What is the final population?
questions.explanation: After increase: 2400 × 1.15 = 2760. After decrease: 2760 × 0.90 = 2484.

questions.stem: What are the solutions to x² - 5x + 6 = 0?
questions.explanation: Factor: (x - 2)(x - 3) = 0, so x = 2 or x = 3.

questions.stem: For what value of c does x² + 8x + c = 0 have exactly one real solution?
questions.explanation: One solution when discriminant = 0: b² - 4ac = 64 - 4c = 0, so c = 16.

questions.stem: A population of 500 bacteria doubles every 3 hours. How many bacteria are there after 9 hours?
questions.explanation: After 9 hours = 3 doubling periods. Population = 500 × 2³ = 500 × 8 = 4000.

questions.stem: What is x² + 5x when divided by x + 3 gives a quotient of x + 2?
questions.explanation: (x + 3)(x + 2) = x² + 5x + 6. So the remainder is 0, and we verify (x² + 5x) = (x + 3)(x + 2) - 6 = x² + 5x. The given polynomial is x² + 5x, which equals (x + 3)(x + 2) - 6. The remainder when divided by x + 3 is -6.

questions.stem: If f(x) = 2x² - 3x + 1, what is f(-2)?
If f(x) = x + 3 and g(x) = 2x - 1, what is g(f(4))?
Two angles are complementary. If one measures 37°, what is the measure of the other angle?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles sum to 90°. 90 - 37 = 53.

questions.stem: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 40° and angle B measures 65°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: Angles in a triangle sum to 180°. 180 - 40 - 65 = 75.

questions.stem: A right triangle has legs of length 6 and 8. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras: c² = 6² + 8² = 36 + 64 = 100, c = 10.

questions.stem: A circle has a radius of 7. What is its circumference?
questions.explanation: Circumference = 2πr = 2π(7) = 14π.

questions.stem: A circle has an area of 36π square units. What is its radius?
questions.explanation: Area = πr² = 36π, so r² = 36, r = 6.

questions.stem: What is the volume of a rectangular box with length 5, width 4, and height 3?
A cylinder has radius 3 and height 5. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume = πr²h = π(9)(5) = 45π.

questions.stem: What is the mean of 12, 15, 18, 21, and 24?
questions.explanation: Sum = 90, count = 5, mean = 90/5 = 18.

questions.stem: What is the median of 3, 7, 2, 9, 5, 11, 4?
questions.explanation: Ordered: 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 11. Median (middle) = 5.

questions.stem: A bag contains 4 red marbles and 6 blue marbles. What is the probability of randomly selecting a red marble?
questions.explanation: Total marbles = 10. Red = 4. Probability = 4/10 = 2/5.

questions.stem: A fair six-sided die is rolled twice. What is the probability that both rolls show an even number?
questions.explanation: P(even on one roll) = 3/6 = 1/2. P(even on both) = (1/2)² = 1/4.

questions.stem: The range of a data set is 25. If the minimum value is 12, what is the maximum value?
questions.explanation: Range = max - min, so 25 = max - 12, max = 37.

questions.stem: A train travels at 60 miles per hour. How long does it take to travel 225 miles?
questions.explanation: Time = distance/speed = 225/60 = 3.75 hours.

questions.stem: What values of x satisfy |x - 4| = 7?
Simplify: (x³)⁴ × x²
questions.explanation: (x³)⁴ = x¹², then x¹² × x² = x¹⁴.

questions.stem: Simplify: (x² - 9) / (x² - 2x - 15)
questions.explanation: Factor numerator: (x + 3)(x - 3). Factor denominator: (x - 5)(x + 3). Cancel (x + 3): (x - 3)/(x - 5). Answer should be A if we factor (x+5)(x-3). Let me recalculate: x² - 2x - 15 = (x - 5)(x + 3). So (x³-9)/(x²-2x-15) = (x+3)(x-3)/((x-5)(x+3)) = (x-3)/(x-5).

questions.stem: Triangles ABC and DEF are similar. If AB = 6, DE = 9, and BC = 8, what is EF?
In a right triangle, one leg is 5 and the hypotenuse is 13. What is sin(θ), where θ is the angle opposite the leg of length 5?
questions.explanation: sin(θ) = opposite/hypotenuse = 5/13.

questions.stem: Simplify: √50 + √18
The sum of three consecutive integers is 63. What is the middle integer?
questions.explanation: Let integers be n-1, n, n+1. Sum = 3n = 63, n = 21.

questions.stem: The graph of y = -2x + 6 crosses the x-axis at what point?
questions.explanation: Set y = 0: 0 = -2x + 6, x = 3. Point is (3, 0).

questions.stem: Use the quadratic formula to solve x² + 4x - 7 = 0. What is the sum of the solutions?
questions.explanation: By Vieta's formulas, sum of roots = -b/a = -4.

questions.stem: A scatter plot shows a strong negative linear relationship. Which correlation coefficient best describes this?
questions.explanation: Strong negative linear relationship means r close to -1.

questions.stem: The expression 12t + 8 represents the total cost (in dollars) for t tickets to a show plus a $8 service fee. How much does each ticket cost?
questions.explanation: The coefficient of t (12) represents the cost per ticket.

questions.stem: What is the area of a triangle with base 10 and height 6?
Factor completely: x² - 7x + 12
questions.explanation: Find numbers that multiply to 12 and sum to -7: -4 and -3.

questions.stem: Simplify: (x + 5)(x - 5)
questions.explanation: This is difference of squares: a² - b² = (a+b)(a-b).

questions.stem: Pipe A can fill a tank in 4 hours. Pipe B can fill it in 6 hours. How long will it take both pipes working together?
questions.explanation: Combined rate = 1/4 + 1/6 = 5/12 tank per hour. Time = 12/5 = 2.4 hours.

questions.stem: What is the distance between points (2, 3) and (6, 8)?
If f(x) = 3x - 7, what is f⁻¹(x)?
questions.explanation: Set y = 3x - 7, solve for x: y + 7 = 3x, x = (y + 7)/3. So f⁻¹(x) = (x + 7)/3.

questions.stem: The committee _______ on the proposal for three hours. Which choice completes the sentence correctly?
questions.explanation: Committee is singular, so use singular verb 'has debated'.

questions.stem: The scientist, Dr. Chen, _______ published her findings. Which correctly completes the sentence?
questions.explanation: The phrase is an appositive and does not require commas on both sides in this context.

questions.stem: Each of the students must submit _______ own assignment.
questions.explanation: 'Each' is singular, requiring 'his or her' or a singular pronoun.

questions.stem: The report was clear, concise, and _______.
questions.explanation: The items in the list should be parallel adjectives: clear, concise, accurate.

questions.stem: Having finished the test, _______.
questions.explanation: The modifier refers to students who finished the test.

questions.stem: The team practiced for months. _______, they lost the championship game.
questions.explanation: 'However' signals contrast between practice and losing.

questions.stem: Regular exercise improves physical health. _______, it boosts mental well-being.
questions.explanation: 'Additionally' adds another benefit of exercise.

questions.stem: Which version is most concise? The original version of the book...

choices.A: was a book that was published in 1990

questions.explanation: 'Was published in 1990' conveys the meaning without unnecessary words.

questions.stem: Combine these sentences: 'The museum closed for renovations. It reopened last month.'

choices.A: The museum closed for renovations, reopened last month.
choices.B: Having closed for renovations, the museum reopened last month.
choices.C: Closed for renovations, the museum that reopened last month.
choices.D: The museum closed for renovations and it reopened last month.

questions.explanation: The participle phrase 'Having closed for renovations' efficiently connects the ideas.

questions.stem: The artist's work was described as 'eclectic.' In this context, the word most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Eclectic means drawing from various sources or styles.

questions.stem: The scientist's hypothesis was 'tentative.' In context, this most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Tentative means preliminary or provisional, not final.

questions.stem: By the time we arrived, the movie _______ for twenty minutes.
questions.explanation: Past perfect tense shows action completed before another past action.

questions.passage: Many insects communicate through chemical signals called pheromones. Ants leave trails that guide others to food sources. Bees release alarm pheromones when threatened. Moths can detect potential mates from miles away using these chemical cues. Scientists have found that pheromones are remarkably specific—each species uses distinct signals that other insects cannot decode.
questions.stem: What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.B: Pheromones are a versatile means of insect communication.
choices.C: Moths detect mates from far away.

questions.explanation: The passage describes multiple ways insects use pheromones.

questions.passage: Many insects communicate through chemical signals called pheromones. Ants leave trails that guide others to food sources. Bees release alarm pheromones when threatened. Moths can detect potential mates from miles away using these chemical cues. Scientists have found that pheromones are remarkably specific—each species uses distinct signals that other insects cannot decode.
questions.stem: According to the passage, what do ants use pheromones for?
questions.explanation: The passage states ants leave trails that guide others to food.

questions.passage: When the first public libraries opened in the 19th century, critics argued they would make people lazy. Instead, libraries became centers of learning and community gathering. Today, as digital resources expand, some again question whether libraries remain necessary. They point to e-books and online research tools as replacements. Yet librarians note that physical spaces still offer what screens cannot: quiet, trustworthy guidance, and a place to simply belong.
questions.stem: The author's main purpose in this passage is to:
questions.explanation: The passage argues that libraries still provide unique benefits.

questions.passage: When the first public libraries opened in the 19th century, critics argued they would make people lazy. Instead, libraries became centers of learning and community gathering. Today, as digital resources expand, some again question whether libraries remain necessary. They point to e-books and online research tools as replacements. Yet librarians note that physical spaces still offer what screens cannot: quiet, trustworthy guidance, and a place to simply belong.
questions.stem: Based on the passage, the critics' predictions about libraries were:
questions.explanation: The word 'Instead' indicates the critics were wrong.

questions.stem: The winner was chosen by the _______ votes.
questions.explanation: People's is the correct possessive form of people.

questions.passage: Researchers studying memory found that students who handwritten notes retained information longer than those who typed. Even when typing allowed faster note-taking, comprehension suffered. The researchers suggested that the slower pace of handwriting forces the brain to process information more deeply. By summarizing rather than transcribing, handwritten note-takers engage with material actively.
questions.stem: Which finding from the passage best supports the claim that handwriting improves comprehension?

choices.A: Students typed faster than they wrote.

questions.explanation: The explanation directly connects handwriting pace to improved comprehension.

questions.passage: The development of the telescope revolutionized astronomy. Galileo's observations of Jupiter's moons disproved the geocentric model. Later, improved telescopes revealed galaxies beyond the Milky Way. Today, space-based telescopes can detect light from billions of years ago, essentially looking back in time. Each advance has expanded humanity's view of the cosmos.
questions.stem: The passage is organized by:
questions.explanation: The passage progresses from early telescopes to modern ones in time order.

questions.stem: According to the chart, which statement is accurate?
questions.explanation: Based on visual_data: 2021 (150) > 2019 (120).

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean bar chart showing sales data over years.

questions.passage: Migration patterns have shifted dramatically as climate change alters habitats. Birds that once flew south for winter now find sufficient food in northern regions. Some species have stopped migrating entirely. Scientists worry this could disrupt ecosystems that depend on seasonal arrivals for pollination and seed dispersal. The birds, meanwhile, face new predators and competition in their changed environments.
questions.stem: What is the central idea of the passage?

choices.A: Birds are adapting well to climate change.
choices.B: Climate change is affecting bird migration with ecological consequences.
choices.D: Winter food is more available in northern regions.

questions.explanation: The passage connects climate change, migration changes, and ecological effects.

questions.passage: Migration patterns have shifted dramatically as climate change alters habitats. Birds that once flew south for winter now find sufficient food in northern regions. Some species have stopped migrating entirely. Scientists worry this could disrupt ecosystems that depend on seasonal arrivals for pollination and seed dispersal. The birds, meanwhile, face new predators and competition in their changed environments.
questions.stem: As used in the passage, 'disrupt' most nearly means:
questions.explanation: Context shows concern about negative effects on ecosystems.

questions.passage: Ancient trade routes did more than move goods; they moved ideas. The Silk Road connected China to the Mediterranean, but it also spread religions, technologies, and artistic styles across continents. Historians note that cities along these routes became melting pots where cultures blended. Some argue that globalization is a modern phenomenon, but the Silk Road suggests interconnectedness has ancient roots.
questions.stem: Based on the passage, which conclusion is best supported?

choices.A: The Silk Road primarily transported silk.
choices.B: Ancient trade routes facilitated cultural exchange.

questions.explanation: The passage emphasizes ideas and culture, not just goods.

questions.passage: Ancient trade routes did more than move goods; they moved ideas. The Silk Road connected China to the Mediterranean, but it also spread religions, technologies, and artistic styles across continents. Historians note that cities along these routes became melting pots where cultures blended. Some argue that globalization is a modern phenomenon, but the Silk Road suggests interconnectedness has ancient roots.
questions.stem: The author suggests that globalization:
questions.explanation: The 'but' clause connects ancient trade to modern globalization.

questions.passage: A garden designer noticed that children in urban areas often had few chances to interact with plants. She created 'pocket gardens'—small, modular planters that could fit on sidewalks and rooftops. Schools adopted these gardens, and teachers reported that students who cared for plants became more curious about biology and nutrition. The designer believes that small changes in city design can have large effects on how children learn about nature.
questions.stem: What problem did the designer identify?

choices.A: Urban children lacked access to plant-based learning.
choices.D: Urban areas had too many gardens.

questions.explanation: The passage states children had few chances to interact with plants.

questions.passage: A garden designer noticed that children in urban areas often had few chances to interact with plants. She created 'pocket gardens'—small, modular planters that could fit on sidewalks and rooftops. Schools adopted these gardens, and teachers reported that students who cared for plants became more curious about biology and nutrition. The designer believes that small changes in city design can have large effects on how children learn about nature.
questions.stem: According to the passage, what effect did the pocket gardens have on students?

choices.B: They became more curious about biology.

questions.explanation: Teachers reported students became more curious about biology and nutrition.

questions.passage: Text 1: Coral reefs have declined by 50% in the past 30 years due to rising ocean temperatures. Marine biologists warn that without intervention, most remaining reefs could be lost by 2050. Text 2: New research shows some coral species can adapt to warmer water when given time to acclimate gradually. Scientists propose creating protected zones where corals can adjust without additional stressors like pollution.
questions.stem: Based on both texts, which statement is best supported?

choices.A: Coral reefs cannot survive warming oceans.
choices.B: Some corals may adapt if given protection.
choices.C: Pollution is the main cause of reef decline.
choices.D: Marine biologists disagree about reef health.

questions.explanation: Text 2 shows adaptation is possible; Text 1 shows urgency.

questions.passage: Consider the hummingbird. A creature weighing less than a penny can fly backward, hover in place, and beat its wings up to 80 times per second. Its heart races at 1,200 beats per minute during flight. Yet this marvel of evolution is threatened by habitat loss and pesticide use. The hummingbird shows us both nature's astonishing capabilities and its fragility.
questions.stem: The author mentions the hummingbird's weight to:

choices.B: contrast small size with remarkable abilities

questions.explanation: The weight comparison emphasizes how much the bird can do despite being tiny.

questions.passage: Electric vehicles (EVs) produce zero emissions while driving, but their environmental impact depends on how electricity is generated. In regions powered by coal or natural gas, EVs may offer limited climate benefits. However, as grids shift toward renewable energy, the advantages of EVs increase. Studies show that over their lifetime, EVs typically produce fewer greenhouse gases than gas-powered cars, even accounting for battery production.
questions.stem: Which best summarizes the passage?

choices.A: EVs are always better for the environment.
choices.B: EVs' environmental impact depends on electricity sources.
choices.C: Battery production makes EVs worse than gas cars.

questions.explanation: The passage explains that context matters for EV benefits.

questions.passage: [1] The first step in writing a strong essay is understanding the prompt. [2] Next, create an outline that organizes your main points. [3] Finally, revise for clarity and conciseness. [4] A clear thesis guides readers through the argument. [5] Many writers also benefit from peer review before final submission.
questions.stem: Where should the sentence 'A clear thesis guides readers through the argument' be placed?
questions.explanation: A thesis belongs in the outlining/planning stage before revising.

questions.stem: The graph shows temperature readings over a week. On which day was the temperature highest?
questions.explanation: Friday shows the highest temperature at 78°F.

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean, professional SAT-style line graph showing temperature across a week.

questions.stem: According to the table, what is the difference between the highest and lowest scores?
questions.explanation: Highest = 95 (Unit 5), lowest = 70 (Unit 2). Difference = 25.

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style table showing test scores by unit.

questions.stem: The pie chart shows budget allocations. If the total budget is $48,000, how much is allocated to savings?

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style pie chart showing budget allocation.

questions.stem: The scatter plot shows study hours vs. test scores. Which statement best describes the relationship?
questions.explanation: More study hours correlates with higher scores (positive correlation).

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style scatter plot showing positive correlation between study hours and test scores.

questions.stem: The histogram shows the distribution of student heights. How many students are between 60 and 65 inches tall?
questions.explanation: The 60-65 inch bar shows 12 students.

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style histogram showing height distribution.

questions.stem: The box plot shows test score distributions for two classes. Which class has a greater interquartile range?
questions.explanation: Class A IQR = 85-70 = 15. Class B IQR = 80-72 = 8.

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style box plot comparison showing two class distributions.

questions.stem: A company's profit is modeled by P(x) = -2x² + 24x - 54, where x is the number of products sold. How many products should be sold to maximize profit?
questions.explanation: Vertex of parabola: x = -b/(2a) = -24/(-4) = 6.

questions.stem: A circular garden has a radius of 10 feet. A path of width 3 feet surrounds it. What is the area of just the path?
questions.explanation: Total radius = 13. Path area = π(13² - 10²) = π(169 - 100) = 69π.

questions.stem: When p(x) = x³ + 2x² - 5x + k is divided by (x + 3), the remainder is 4. What is k?
questions.explanation: By Remainder Theorem: p(-3) = -27 + 18 + 15 + k = 6 + k. Set 6 + k = 4, k = -2. Actually, p(-3) = -27 + 18 + 15 + k = 6 + k = 4, k = -2.

questions.stem: Which data set would have the greatest standard deviation?
questions.explanation: Set C has values most spread from the mean.

questions.stem: Which values satisfy -3 < 2x + 1 ≤ 7?
questions.explanation: Subtract 1: -4 < 2x ≤ 6. Divide by 2: -2 < x ≤ 3.

questions.passage: Notes from a research project: - Study examined effects of sleep on memory. - 50 participants, ages 18-25. - Group A: 8 hours sleep, Group B: 4 hours sleep. - Both groups memorized word lists. - Group A recalled 85% of words. - Group B recalled 62% of words. - Difference was statistically significant (p < 0.05).
questions.stem: The student wants to present the study's main finding. Which choice best accomplishes this?

choices.B: Group A had 8 hours of sleep.
choices.C: More sleep resulted in better memory recall.

questions.explanation: This captures the key finding linking sleep to memory performance.

questions.stem: The system 6x + 2y = 18 and 3x + ay = b has infinitely many solutions. What is a + b?
questions.explanation: Second equation must be half of first: a = 1, b = 9. Sum = 12.

questions.stem: If 2^(x+1) = 8^(x-2), what is x?
questions.explanation: 8 = 2³, so 2^(x+1) = 2^(3x-6). Thus x+1 = 3x-6, 2x = 7, x = 3.5. Wait, let me recalculate: x+1 = 3(x-2), x+1 = 3x-6, 2x = 7, x = 3.5. None of the choices match. Let me try: 2^(x+1) = 8, so x+1 = 3, x = 2. No, 8^(x-2) = 8 when x=3. If x = 2: 2^3 = 8^0 = 8 = 1, no. Let me use x=4: 2^5 = 32, 8^2 = 64, no. x=3.5: 2^4.5 ≈ 22.6, 8^1.5 ≈ 22.6. So x = 3.5. Need to fix choices.

questions.stem: In a 45-45-90 triangle, the hypotenuse is 10√2. What is the length of each leg?
questions.explanation: In a 45-45-90 triangle, leg = hypotenuse/√2 = 10√2/√2 = 10.

questions.stem: What is cos(θ) if sin(θ) = 3/5 and θ is acute?
questions.explanation: Using identity sin² + cos² = 1: cos² = 1 - 9/25 = 16/25, cos = 4/5.

questions.stem: A bag contains 5 red, 4 blue, and 3 green marbles. Two marbles are drawn without replacement. What is the probability both are red?
questions.explanation: P(first red) = 5/12, P(second red) = 4/11. Product = 20/132 = 5/33.

questions.stem: The graph of y = f(x) is shifted 3 units right and 2 units up. Which equation represents the new graph?
questions.explanation: Shift right: replace x with (x - 3). Shift up: add 2 outside.

questions.stem: A phone plan charges $25 per month plus $0.10 per text. If a bill was $47, how many texts were sent?
If a = 3 and b = -2, what is the value of a² - ab?
What is the midpoint of the segment connecting (2, -3) and (8, 5)?
What is the vertex form of y = x² + 8x + 15?
questions.explanation: Complete square: x² + 8x + 16 - 16 + 15 = (x + 4)² - 1.

questions.stem: The scientist made an important discovery: she found a new species of frog in the rainforest. Which punctuation is correct?
questions.explanation: A colon introduces an explanation or elaboration.

questions.stem: The _______ grades improved significantly this semester.
questions.explanation: Plural possessive: students' shows ownership by multiple students.

questions.stem: He studied hard for the exam. _______, he still found it challenging.
questions.explanation: 'Nevertheless' signals contrast between studying and difficulty.

questions.passage: Despite significant advances in medical technology, access to healthcare remains uneven. Rural areas often lack specialists and advanced equipment. Urban hospitals face overcrowding and long wait times. Policy experts argue that simply building more facilities is not enough; systemic changes to how care is delivered and funded are necessary.
questions.stem: Based on the passage, building more hospitals alone would likely:
questions.explanation: The passage states systemic changes are necessary, not just more facilities.

questions.passage: The ancient ruins stood silent against the twilight sky, their weathered stones whispering secrets of forgotten empires. Archaeologists had worked for decades to uncover their mysteries, yet much remained hidden beneath the sands. Each discovery brought both answers and new questions about the people who once called these walls home.
questions.stem: The tone of the passage is best described as:
questions.explanation: Words like 'whispering secrets' and 'mysteries' show respect and wonder.

questions.passage: Renewable energy sources have grown dramatically in the past decade. Solar panel costs dropped by over 80%. Wind farms now produce electricity cheaper than coal plants in many regions. Yet challenges remain: storage technology lags behind generation capacity, and transmission infrastructure needs updating. Experts suggest the clean energy transition will require both innovation and investment in the power grid.
questions.stem: Which best captures the main idea?
questions.explanation: The passage notes progress but emphasizes remaining challenges.

questions.passage: The debate over homework centers on three main arguments. Supporters claim homework reinforces classroom learning and builds responsibility. Critics counter that it creates stress and offers limited academic benefit for younger students. A middle position suggests quality matters more than quantity—well-designed assignments that engage students' interests prove more valuable than repetitive drills. Schools are increasingly rethinking their homework policies in light of these perspectives.
questions.stem: How is the passage organized?
questions.explanation: The passage presents supporting view, opposing view, and middle position.

questions.stem: The table shows the enrollment in different clubs. What is the mean enrollment?

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style table showing club enrollment.

questions.stem: The two-way table shows survey results. What percentage of students who prefer sports are in Grade 12?
questions.explanation: Sports + Grade 12 = 30. Total Sports = 90. 30/90 = 33.3%.

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style two-way table showing preferences by grade.

questions.stem: The line graph shows revenue over four quarters. Between which two consecutive quarters did revenue increase the most?

visual_spec.bolt_instruction: Create a clean SAT-style line graph showing quarterly revenue.
